response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to one early-career position (NI LabWindows/CVI components) and a university thesis project, far short of the mandated 5–10 years of recent, hands-on Windows C++ work. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (only MySQL/MongoDB), and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain exposure — the closest is a Node.js role at a German investment platform, which is retail fintech rather than institutional capital markets. Their depth is in TypeScript/Node, DLang, and web/full-stack systems, making them a poor fit for this specific engagement.
strengths: Solid low-latency and systems programming background (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput microservices)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching
Broad, adaptable technology range across backend, desktop, mobile, and embedded
concerns: C++ experience is dated and brief, not the required 5–10 recent years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets/investment banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain experience
Strong general systems and performance engineering skills
